meta.name: Half Marathon Novice
meta.subtitle: Your first 21.1km — a 16-week journey
meta.description: For runners who can cover 10km and want to attempt their first half marathon. Sixteen weeks of progressive long-run building, with run/walk strategies available early in the plan for those who need them.
meta.coach_notes: The half marathon is a serious undertaking for a first-timer — not because it is beyond you, but because it demands respect. The long run on Sunday is the session that builds race-day confidence. Do not skip it. Do not run it too fast. Everything else in the week supports it.

weeks.title: Week 1
weeks.note: First week. Long run is the priority. Keep everything else easy and genuinely so — you should be able to hold a full conversation on all sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:20/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 6km easy · Middle 2km slightly quicker · Comfortable — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 7km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
10km easy · 6:10–6:30/km · This is the session that matters most this week — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Week 2
weeks.note: Long run reaches 12km. Keep pace the same as last week — the goal is time on feet, not pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 7km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 6km steady · 5:45–6:00/km for middle 3km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
12km easy · 6:10–6:30/km · Walk if needed — the distance matters, not continuous running

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Week 3
weeks.note: 13km long run. If the last 2km of your long run are very difficult, your pace is too fast. Slow down.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 7km · Middle 3km at steady effort — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
13km easy · 6:10–6:30/km — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Recovery week. Volume drops. Long run stays manageable.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

weeks.note: The long run is now at half-marathon distance for some athletes. 15km is the longest training run of the first block.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

weeks.title: Week 9
weeks.note: Race-specific phase begins. The Thursday run now includes sustained effort at race pace (6:00–6:15/km for a sub-2:10 half).

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km · Middle 4km at race pace (6:00–6:15/km) · This is what race day will feel like — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

weeks.title: Week 10
weeks.note: Long run reaches 18km — furthest of the plan. Keep the pace easy. Walk through water stations and uphills if needed — that is race strategy, not failure.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 9km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

18km easy · 6:10–6:30/km · Optional: gel at 12km to practise race nutrition — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Week 11
weeks.note: Peak week. 19km long run on Sunday is the furthest you will run before race day. If you can cover 19km in training, 21.1km on race day is absolutely within you.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 9km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km · 5km at race pace within run — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

19km easy · Practise gel at 12km · This is your confidence-building run — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Week 12
weeks.note: Volume reduces as we approach the race. Keep the Thursday session at race pace — this builds confidence.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 7km · 4km at race pace — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 6:00–6:30/km · Conversational — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

sessions.det: 7km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 6km · Middle 3km at race pace — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

weeks.note: Second taper week. Legs should start feeling fresh.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 6km easy · With 4x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

sessions.det: 5km · 2km at race pace — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

sessions.det: 5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
9km easy · Stay fresh — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.

weeks.title: Race week prep
weeks.note: Week before race week. Keep moving but nothing hard.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 4km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 4km easy · 2x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 3km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
4km easy · Arrive at race week feeling great — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey. 600ml water. 90–120 minutes before. Gel at 12km+ for runs over 90 minutes.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. Protein, carbohydrates, vegetables. This is your most important weekly nutrition window.

weeks.title: Race week — first half marathon
weeks.note: Race week. You have done the work. Trust it.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 3km easy · 4x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Very light — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

nut.d: 500ml water. Rest day — recovery is training.

sessions.det: 2km easy · Stay loose — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

10min walk warm-up · 4x100m strides · Race: first 5km at 6:15/km, settle to goal pace, walk through stations · Walk/run mix is completely acceptable · Finish is all that matters — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana and honey, or two slices of toast with peanut butter. 600ml water. Stick entirely to foods you have eaten before long runs — half marathon nutrition starts with breakfast.
300ml water or isotonic drink. Do not gulp — sip steadily. If you normally use caffeine, this is the time.
10–15min easy jog. 4x100m strides. The warm-up is essential for a half marathon — you will not be running yourself into it in the first mile.
Gel with water at the mile 6 or 7 station. Take it with a full cup of water, not a sip. If you have not trained with gels, take water only.
Second gel at mile 10 if you trained with two gels. Otherwise water only. Do not take a gel you have not practised with.
Water or isotonic drink at the finish line. 500ml in the first 10 minutes. Keep moving — walk for at least 10 minutes to flush lactic acid.
Banana and protein bar or shake. Start the recovery process immediately — you have just run 21km.
Within 90 minutes: protein (30–40g), carbohydrates, vegetables. 1.5g protein per kg bodyweight today. Drink consistently through the afternoon — 500ml extra per hour for the first 3 hours.
